message:
fix(fr-command): XADD MAXLEN+MINID conflict + LIMIT validation per upstream Redis 7+ (br-frankenredis-r71v)

Upstream t_stream.c::streamParseAddOrTrimArgsOrReply enforces three
validation rules our XADD parser was missing:

  1. MAXLEN and MINID are mutually exclusive. Combo returns
     "ERR syntax error, MAXLEN and MINID options at the same time
     are not compatible".
  2. The LIMIT keyword (Redis 7.0+) is parsed after MAXLEN/MINID
     and bounds the trim sweep. We never recognized it. Treating
     LIMIT as an unknown token misparsed it as a stream ID,
     leading to "Invalid stream ID" instead of upstream's specific
     wording.
  3. LIMIT requires a trimming strategy AND the approximate (~)
     form. Without strategy: "ERR syntax error, LIMIT cannot be
     used without specifying a trimming strategy". Without ~:
     "ERR syntax error, LIMIT cannot be used without the special
     ~ option". Negative LIMIT: "ERR The LIMIT argument must be
     >= 0.".

We retain the existing best-effort no-op semantic for
approximate (~) trimming — upstream's lazy approximate trim is
a may-skip contract, and our no-op is one valid implementation.
This preserves the pre-existing
xadd_nomkstream_and_approx_maxlen_match_current_redis_behavior
test case.

Two new fr-command tests pin the upstream wording verbatim:
  * xadd_maxlen_minid_combo_rejected_with_upstream_wording
  * xadd_limit_requires_trim_strategy_and_approx (4 sub-cases)

fr-command 641 passed; live-oracle conformance 183 passed
under STRICT mode.

Slice of br-frankenredis-r71v (Redis 7+ surface reality-check).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
